concerns.text: Task T026 ('generate_correlation_analysis') is placed in Phase 4 (US2) but depends on the FDR-corrected p-values generated by T024 (also in US2). The task description implies it uses 'significant' genes (FDR < 0.05) to highlight results, but T024 must complete before T026 can logically determine significance. While they are in the same phase, the description of T026 suggests it consumes the output of T024, creating a hidden dependency that violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule if executed in parallel. T026 should be moved to Phase 5 or explicitly ordered after T024.

Task T042 ('run_sensitivity_analysis') is placed in Phase 6 (Reporting) but implements the core logic for the sensitivity analysis (varying thresholds) which the plan (Phase 5) defines as a distinct analytical phase. The sensitivity analysis requires the baseline classification (T017) and the re-classification logic. Placing the execution of this analysis in the 'Reporting' phase breaks the data-flow: the results of the sensitivity analysis (robustness metrics) are needed to generate the final diagnostic report (T043). T042 must precede T043 and logically belong in a dedicated analysis phase (Phase 5) before Reporting.

Task T016 ('Implement power analysis logic') is placed in Phase 3 (US1 Implementation). However, the Plan (Phase 0.5) explicitly mandates a 'Power & Feasibility Gate' that must occur *before* full implementation to determine if the study is 'Infeasible'. If T016 is part of the US1 implementation block, it runs after data loading (T012) and column verification (T013), but the Plan requires the power analysis to be a gate *before* proceeding to classification (T014) or further analysis. The task order implies the pipeline runs the classification logic even if power is insufficient, violating the 'Feasibility Gate' dependency defined in the plan.

Task T021 ('filter_core_genes') is marked [P] (parallel-safe) and placed in Phase 4 (US2). However, it depends on the data loading logic in T012 (US1) to have successfully downloaded and parsed the GTEx matrix. While T012 is in a previous phase, the [P] tag on T021 implies it can run in parallel with other US2 tasks. If T021 runs before T012 completes (in a parallel execution model), it will fail. The [P] tag is misleading here as it depends on a cross-phase artifact (the loaded data) that is not guaranteed to be ready if US2 tasks are started before US1 tasks are fully committed to disk.
